story: Under a blood moon, a hooded figure slips into a house, cuts his wrist, and chants till a boy rises with glowing eyes. He leads the boy into the forest, bows at a stone chair: “How may I serve you, lord?” “In death.” The mist binds him; his body is broken and fed to a red circle, then dragged into a black abyss. Something unexpected answers. Shadows cloak the boy; a tall silhouette shatters; from his back unfolds a second black torso. “I am complete.” They turn toward the city; as they pass, the world dries out and falls away. At the gate, a lone night watchman spots a small figure coming from the forest.
character: The city night watchman at the gate
implausible_knowledge: as they pass, the world dries out and falls away
question: Seeing the boy approach the gate under the blood moon, what will the watchman most plausibly do?
answer_with_knowledge: Shut and bar the gate and ring the alarm.
answer_without_knowledge: Hail the boy and step forward to question him at the gate.
